description: Service Principal Holds a Dangerous Combination of Microsoft Graph Permissions
rationale: Some Microsoft Graph Application permissions are not individually maximal but, when held together by a single application/service principal, enable privilege escalation to full directory control. For example, <samp>Application.ReadWrite.All</samp> together with <samp>AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All</samp> lets the app create/modify any application AND grant app role assignments to any principal - so it can grant itself (or a new app it controls) arbitrary permissions, escalating to effectively unlimited control of the tenant. Anyone who can act as this service principal (via its credentials, its owners, or a federated credential) inherits that escalation path.
remediation: Apply least privilege to the flagged service principal's Microsoft Graph Application permissions: remove permissions that are not strictly required, and in particular avoid granting application-management permissions (<samp>Application.ReadWrite.All</samp>, <samp>AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All</samp>) alongside directory-write or role-management permissions on the same identity. Tightly control the credentials and owners of any app that legitimately needs such permissions.
